channel.title: Cadence 步频 — PT Research Signal
channel.description: Daily curated physical therapy & rehab research for clinicians. AI-scored signal from 50 sources.

item.title: Beyond "Biomedical-Plus": Reclaiming the biopsychosocial model in musculoskeletal physiotherapy.
item.description: This article argues that musculoskeletal physiotherapy's implementation of the biopsychosocial model often remains a "biomedical-plus" approach, suggesting a need to focus on therapist confidence, role clarity, and compassionate communication for true integration.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Patient Engagement, Clinical Outcomes, and Satisfaction of an Advanced Orthopedic Virtual Physical Therapy Model: A Retrospective Cohort Study.
item.description: A retrospective study of an advanced orthopedic virtual PT model found high patient engagement (62.8% evaluated, 70.9% completed care), with 96% of completers achieving MCID and high satisfaction, exceeding national benchmarks.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Effectiveness of Combined Biopsychosocial Interventions for Chronic Low Back Pain: A Systematic Review and Meta-analysis of Randomized Controlled Trials.
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis of RCTs found combined biopsychosocial interventions to be effective in reducing pain and functional disability in adults with chronic low back pain compared to usual care, waiting-list controls, and active unimodal interventions.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Comparative efficacy of different joint mobilization techniques in shoulder adhesive capsulitis: a systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials.
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis of RCTs compared the clinical effects of different non-thrust joint mobilization approaches for shoulder adhesive capsulitis on objective and subjective outcomes.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: APTA, Hartford HealthCare Launch Frailty Pilot to Advance Value-Based Care
item.description: APTA and Hartford HealthCare launched a pilot program to test an early frailty identification and intervention model, aiming to advance value-based care and position physical therapists at the forefront of prevention for aging populations.
item.author: APTA

item.title: Virtual Reality-assisted Physiotherapeutic Training for Patients With Knee Osteoarthritis: A Systematic Review and Meta-analysis.
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis of 5 RCTs found that virtual reality (VR)-assisted physiotherapy significantly improved pain (SMD = 0.40, 95% CI: 0.14-0.65, P = 0.002), physical function (SMD = 0.37, 95% CI: 0.05-0.70, P = 0.003), and balance (SMD = 0.44, 95% CI: 0.16-0.72, P = 0.002) in patients with knee osteoarthritis compared to traditional rehabilitation.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Association of combined physical and cognitive rehabilitation with post-stroke dementia: a U.S. multicenter retrospective cohort study.
item.description: A U.S. multicenter retrospective cohort study compared combined physical and cognitive rehabilitation (PR+CR) versus PR alone in reducing post-stroke dementia risk among 479 propensity score-matched stroke patients aged ≥55 years with MCI. The study found a significant association between PR+CR and a reduced risk of incident post-stroke dementia.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: The Oxford visual perception screen detects visual perception difficulties in sub-acute adult stroke survivors: an early cross-sectional diagnostic accuracy study.
item.description: A prospective cross-sectional study evaluated the Oxford Visual Perception Screen (OxVPS) for detecting visual perception difficulties in sub-acute stroke survivors (within 6 weeks of stroke). The OxVPS showed good diagnostic accuracy compared to the Rivermead Perceptual Assessment Battery (RPAB).
item.author: PubMed

item.title: The Impact of Mandatory Bundled Payment on Prehabilitation Delivery for Lower Extremity Joint Replacement.
item.description: A difference-in-differences analysis using Medicare claims data (n=62,217 beneficiaries) found that the Comprehensive Care for Joint Replacement (CJR) mandatory bundled payment model did not significantly increase the proportion of hospitals delivering prehabilitation for lower extremity joint replacement in the U.S.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Aspirin Matches Rivaroxaban for Blood Clot Prevention After Joint Replacement, Suggests Study
item.description: The EPCAT III randomized trial found an aspirin-only regimen was as effective and safe as rivaroxaban for preventing symptomatic venous thromboembolism after total hip or knee arthroplasty, with no clinically significant difference in bleeding risk.
item.author: Medical Dialogues

item.title: Measurement Characteristics and Clinical Utility of the 6-Minute Walk Test Among People with Stroke
item.description: This study focuses on the measurement characteristics and clinical utility of the 6-Minute Walk Test (6MWT) in individuals with stroke.
item.author: Archives of PM&R

item.title: The RESOLVE Trial: A Stepped-Wedge, Cluster-Randomized Clinical Trial Evaluating the Effects of Clinical Practice Guidelines and Psychologically Informed Physical Therapy on Low Back Pain Outcomes in Service Members and Veterans.
item.description: A stepped-wedge, cluster-randomized trial evaluating a clinical practice guideline and psychologically informed physical therapy (CPG+PIPT) implementation strategy found it reduced LBP-related disability, pain, and healthcare utilization in military and veteran settings.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: [Correspondence] From targets to minutes: making movement advice actionable
item.description: This correspondence suggests shifting physical activity messaging from aspirational thresholds to achievable marginal gains, showing that small increases in activity among the least active can yield the greatest population benefit, aligning with WHO guidance.
item.author: The Lancet

item.title: Outpatient rehabilitation can prevent suicide after acquired brain injury.
item.description: An outpatient rehabilitation program for adults with acquired brain injuries (591 patients) was associated with a lower frequency of suicides compared to a matched control group (2,932 controls), suggesting a potential preventive effect.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Transcranial Direct Current Stimulation (tDCS) Application in Neurorehabilitation: A Beginner’s Guide for Clinicians
item.description: This beginner's guide for clinicians explains transcranial direct current stimulation (tDCS) as a noninvasive brain stimulation technique for neurorehabilitation, noting its low cost, small footprint, and ease of combination with therapy.
item.author: Archives of PM&R

item.title: High-intensity interval training in cancer patients: a bayesian meta-analysis of effects on VO2max, dose–response, and adherence | BMC Sports Science, Medicine and Rehabilitation
item.description: A Bayesian meta-analysis on high-intensity interval training (HIIT) in cancer patients investigated its effects on VO2max, dose-response, and adherence, confirming positive impacts and good patient tolerance.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: A Multimodal Assessment of Gastric Motility, reflux risk, and Clinical Efficacy: Intermittent Oro-Esophageal versus Nasogastric Tube Feeding for Post-Stroke Dysphagia in a Randomized Controlled Trial.
item.description: A single-center RCT (n=54) compared intermittent oro-esophageal tube feeding (IOE) with continuous nasogastric tube feeding (NG) for post-stroke dysphagia. The study found IOE to be more effective in reducing reflux risk and supporting swallowing recovery.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Long-term benefits of ultrasound-guided percutaneous electrical nerve stimulation for chronic lateral elbow pain: a randomized controlled trial.
item.description: A randomized, double-blinded, sham-controlled trial evaluating ultrasound-guided percutaneous electrical nerve stimulation (PENS) combined with exercise for chronic lateral elbow pain found significant long-term benefits in pain intensity, pain-related disability, and psychological outcomes compared to sham PENS.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Moderate, Home-Based Exercise Proven Safe for Aortic Dissection Survivors, find researchers
item.description: Researchers have found that moderate, home-based exercise is safe for survivors of aortic dissection, providing important insights for their rehabilitation and long-term management.
item.author: Medical Dialogues

item.title: Exploring equity in telehealth interventions in physiotherapy: A meta-epidemiological study.
item.description: A meta-epidemiological study of 94 telehealth equity trials in physiotherapy found that while telehealth interventions are increasingly studied, equity considerations, especially regarding socioeconomic status and social background, remain a gap.
item.author: PubMed
